docs.title: Session and Memory
docs.text: This part covers session management, memory architecture, and context assembly for OpenClaw agents.

docs.title: Contents
docs.text:
1. Session Management - Session lifecycle and isolation
  2. Memory System - Hierarchical memory architecture
  3. Context Engine - Context assembly and token budgeting
  4. Memory Compaction - Automatic memory optimization

docs.title: Session Key Architecture
docs.text:
Sessions use a structured key format for routing and isolation:

// Session key format
type SessionKey =
  | "main"                                // Global shared session
  | `agent:${AgentId}:${Rest}`            // Agent-scoped session
  | `agent:${AgentId}:${Channel}:...`     // Channel-specific
  | `agent:${AgentId}:global`              // Global per-agent

// Key parsing
interface ParsedAgentSessionKey {
  agentId: string;
  rest: string;        // Everything after agentId
  accountId?: string;  // Optional account scope
}

docs.title: Session Resolution
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    A[Incoming Request] --> B{Parse Session Key}
    B -->|main| C[Global Session]
    B -->|agent:xxx| D[Agent Session]
    D --> E{DM Scope Mode}
    E -->|main| F[agent:xxx:main]
    E -->|per-peer| G[agent:xxx:direct:peer]
    E -->|per-channel-peer| H[agent:xxx:channel:direct:peer]
    E -->|per-account-channel-peer| I[agent:xxx:channel:account:direct:peer]

docs.title: DM Scope Modes
docs.text: Mode Key Format Isolation main agent:id:main Single shared session per-peer agent:id:direct:peer Per user across channels per-channel-peer agent:id:channel:direct:peer Per user per channel per-account-channel-peer agent:id:channel:account:direct:peer Per account+channel+user

docs.title: Group Sessions
docs.text:
Group sessions follow similar patterns with peer kind:

// Group key structure
interface GroupSessionKey {
  channel: string;      // "telegram", "discord"
  accountId?: string;    // Optional account scope
  peerKind: "group" | "channel";
  peerId: string;        // Platform-specific group ID
}

// Example keys
"telegram:group:chat123"
"discord:account1:group:channel456"

docs.title: Thread Sessions
docs.text:
Threads extend base sessions with a suffix:

// Thread session key
const baseKey = "agent:main:telegram:direct:user123";
const threadKey = `${baseKey}:thread:thread456`;

// Parent session tracking
interface ThreadSession {
  sessionKey: string;
  parentSessionKey?: string;
  threadId: string;
}

docs.title: Three-Tier Model
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    subgraph Tier1["Working Memory"]
        Current[Current Turn]
        Recent[Recent Messages]
    end

    subgraph Tier2["Short-term Memory (Session)"]
        MEMORY[MEMORY.md]
        DREAMS[DREAMS.md]
    end

    subgraph Tier3["Long-term Memory (Persistent)"]
        Wiki[Wiki Pages]
        Facts[Inferred Facts]
        Archive[Archived Sessions]
    end

    Tier1 --> Tier2
    Tier2 --> Tier3
    Tier3 -.-> Tier1

docs.title: Memory Files
docs.text: File Purpose Content MEMORY.md Session context Current tasks, ongoing work DREAMS.md Inferred knowledge Model reflections, facts memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md Daily archives Session summaries memory/archive/ Long-term storage Compacted sessions

docs.title: Memory Entry Types
docs.text:
type MemoryType =
  | "fact"        // Inferred from conversation
  | "task"        // Task or goal
  | "preference"  // User preference
  | "knowledge"   // General knowledge
  | "context"     // Conversation context
  | "summary"     // Compaction summary
  | "reflection"  // Model reflection
  | "commitment"; // Stated commitment

interface MemoryEntry {
  id: string;
  key: string;
  type: MemoryType;
  content: string;
  metadata: MemoryMetadata;
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
  embedding?: number[];
}

docs.title: Build Pipeline
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[Query] --> B[Search Memory]
    B --> C[Fetch Entries]
    C --> D[Rank by Relevance]
    D --> E[Build Context]
    E --> F[Budget Tokens]
    F --> G[Inject into Prompt]

docs.title: Token Budgeting
docs.text:
interface ContextBudget {
  totalLimit: number;        // Model context window
  reserved: {
    system: number;          // System prompt
    tools: number;           // Tool definitions
    output: number;          // Response buffer
  };
  available: number;         // For context
  used: number;
}

// Example budgets
const budgets = {
  "gpt-4o": { totalLimit: 128000, available: 100000 },
  "claude-opus-4": { totalLimit: 200000, available: 180000 },
};

docs.title: Context Injection
docs.text:
Context is assembled from multiple sources:

interface ContextAssembly {
  memory: MemoryEntry[];     // Retrieved memories
  recentMessages: Message[]; // Recent conversation
  systemPrompt: string;      // Base system prompt
  agentContext: AgentContext; // Agent-specific data
  tools: ToolDefinition[];   // Available tools
}

docs.title: Compaction Triggers
docs.text: Trigger Condition Token limit Exceeds threshold Idle No activity for period Manual Explicit /compact command Scheduled Daily cron job

docs.title: Compaction Strategies
docs.text:
type CompactionStrategy =
  | { type: "summarize"; maxTokens: number }
  | { type: "selective"; keepTypes: MemoryType[] }
  | { type: "archive"; archiveOlderThan: number }
  | { type: "compress"; algorithm: "gzip" | "lz4" };

docs.title: Compaction Pipeline
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Trigger
    participant Analyzer
    participant Summarizer
    participant Archiver

    Trigger->>Analyzer: should_compact()
    Analyzer-->>Trigger: true
    Trigger->>Analyzer: fetch_old_entries()
    Analyzer-->>Summarizer: entries
    Summarizer->>Summarizer: generate_summary()
    Summarizer->>Archiver: archive(original)
    Summarizer->>Trigger: summary
    Trigger->>Trigger: replace_old_with_summary()

docs.title: Memory Isolation
docs.text:
Each agent has isolated memory:

// Agent-scoped memory paths
"~/.openclaw/agents/{agentId}/memory/"

docs.title: Shared Memory
docs.text:
Agents can opt into shared memory:

config: {
  agents: {
    sharedMemory: {
      enabled: true,
      scope: "team",  // or "global"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Agent Architecture - Agent system
  • Gateway Core - Gateway architecture
  • Config System - Configuration

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Context Engine is a pluggable system responsible for assembling model context within token budgets. It manages the flow from session history to model input, handling retrieval, ranking, truncation, and injection.

flowchart TB
    subgraph Input["Input Sources"]
        Session[Session History]
        Memory[Memory Retrieval]
        System[System Prompt]
        Tools[Tool Definitions]
    end

    subgraph Engine["Context Engine"]
        Ingest[Ingest]
        Assemble[Assemble]
        Maintain[Maintain]
    end

    subgraph Output["Output"]
        Prompt[Model Prompt]
        TokenBudget[Token Budget]
    end

    Session --> Ingest
    Memory --> Ingest
    Ingest --> Assemble
    System --> Assemble
    Tools --> Assemble
    Assemble --> Prompt
    Assemble --> TokenBudget

docs.title: ContextEngine Contract
docs.text:
interface ContextEngine {
  readonly info: ContextEngineInfo;

  // Lifecycle
  bootstrap?(params: BootstrapParams): Promise<BootstrapResult>;
  maintain?(params: MaintainParams): Promise<ContextEngineMaintenanceResult>;

  // Message handling
  ingest(params: IngestParams): Promise<IngestResult>;
  ingestBatch?(params: IngestBatchParams): Promise<IngestBatchResult>;
  afterTurn?(params: AfterTurnParams): Promise<void>;

  // Context assembly
  assemble(params: AssembleParams): Promise<AssembleResult>;

  // Compaction
  compact(params: CompactParams): Promise<CompactResult>;

  // Subagent support
  prepareSubagentSpawn?(params: SubagentSpawnParams): Promise<SubagentSpawnPreparation | undefined>;
  onSubagentEnded?(params: { childSessionKey: string; reason: SubagentEndReason }): Promise<void>;

  // Cleanup
  dispose?(): Promise<void>;
}

docs.title: Engine Info
docs.text:
interface ContextEngineInfo {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  version?: string;
  /** True when the engine manages its own compaction lifecycle. */
  ownsCompaction?: boolean;
  /** Controls how turn-triggered maintenance should be executed. */
  turnMaintenanceMode?: "foreground" | "background";
}

docs.title: The assemble() Method
docs.text:
The primary method for building model context:

interface AssembleParams {
  sessionId: string;
  sessionKey?: string;
  messages: AgentMessage[];
  tokenBudget?: number;
  /** Tool names available for this run. */
  availableTools?: Set<string>;
  /** Active memory citation mode. */
  citationsMode?: MemoryCitationsMode;
  /** Current model identifier. */
  model?: string;
  /** The incoming user prompt for this turn. */
  prompt?: string;
}

interface AssembleResult {
  /** Ordered messages to use as model context */
  messages: AgentMessage[];
  /** Estimated total tokens in assembled context */
  estimatedTokens: number;
  /**
   * Controls which token estimate the runner treats as authoritative.
   * - "assembled": uses only the assembled prompt's estimate
   * - "preassembly_may_overflow": uses maximum of assembled and pre-assembly estimates
   */
  promptAuthority?: "assembled" | "preassembly_may_overflow";
  /** Optional context-engine-provided instructions prepended to system prompt */
  systemPromptAddition?: string;
  /** Projection lifecycle for hosts with persistent backend threads */
  contextProjection?: ContextEngineProjection;
}

docs.title: Token Budget Calculation
docs.text:
interface ContextBudget {
  totalLimit: number;        // Model context window
  reserved: {
    system: number;          // System prompt tokens
    tools: number;          // Tool definitions tokens
    output: number;         // Response buffer tokens
  };
  available: number;        // For context assembly
  used: number;             // Current estimate
}

// Example budgets by model
const budgets: Record<string, number> = {
  "claude-opus-4-7": 200000,
  "claude-sonnet-4.7": 200000,
  "gpt-4o": 128000,
  "gpt-4o-mini": 128000,
};

docs.title: Context Assembly Pipeline
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Caller
    participant Engine
    participant Memory
    participant Budget

    Caller->>Engine: assemble(params)
    Engine->>Budget: calculateBudget(tokenBudget)
    Budget-->>Engine: budget
    Engine->>Memory: searchRelevant(query)
    Memory-->>Engine: entries
    Engine->>Engine: rankAndFilter(entries, budget)
    Engine->>Engine: buildPrompt(messages, memory, budget)
    Engine-->>Caller: AssembleResult

docs.title: Single Message Ingest
docs.text:
interface IngestParams {
  sessionId: string;
  sessionKey?: string;
  message: AgentMessage;
  /** True when the message belongs to a heartbeat run. */
  isHeartbeat?: boolean;
}

interface IngestResult {
  /** Whether the message was ingested (false if duplicate or no-op) */
  ingested: boolean;
}

docs.title: Batch Ingest
docs.text:
For efficiency, multiple messages can be ingested together:

interface IngestBatchParams {
  sessionId: string;
  sessionKey?: string;
  messages: AgentMessage[];
  isHeartbeat?: boolean;
}

interface IngestBatchResult {
  /** Number of messages ingested from the supplied batch */
  ingestedCount: number;
}

docs.title: Bootstrap Flow
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    A[Session Start] --> B{Already Bootstrapped?}
    B -->|yes| C[Skip Bootstrap]
    B -->|no| D[Check Import Needed]
    D -->|yes| E[Import History]
    D -->|no| F[Initialize Fresh]
    E --> G[Engine Ready]
    F --> G
    C --> H[Assemble Context]

docs.title: Bootstrap Parameters
docs.text:
interface BootstrapParams {
  sessionId: string;
  sessionKey?: string;
  sessionFile: string;
}

interface BootstrapResult {
  /** Whether bootstrap ran and initialized the engine's store */
  bootstrapped: boolean;
  /** Number of historical messages imported (if applicable) */
  importedMessages?: number;
  /** Optional reason when bootstrap was skipped */
  reason?: string;
}

docs.title: Turn Maintenance
docs.text:
After each turn, engines can perform maintenance tasks:

interface MaintainParams {
  sessionId: string;
  sessionKey?: string;
  sessionFile: string;
  runtimeContext?: ContextEngineRuntimeContext;
}

interface ContextEngineRuntimeContext {
  /** True when opted into consuming deferred compaction debt */
  allowDeferredCompactionExecution?: boolean;
  /** Runtime-resolved context window budget for the active model call */
  tokenBudget?: number;
  /** Best-effort current prompt/context token estimate */
  currentTokenCount?: number;
  /** Prompt-cache telemetry for cache-aware engines */
  promptCache?: ContextEnginePromptCacheInfo;
  /** Safe transcript rewrite helper */
  rewriteTranscriptEntries?: (
    request: TranscriptRewriteRequest,
  ) => Promise<TranscriptRewriteResult>;
  /** LLM completion capability */
  llm?: {
    complete: (params: LlmCompleteParams) => Promise<LlmCompleteResult>;
  };
}

docs.title: Transcript Rewrites
docs.text:
Engines can request safe branch-and-reappend transcript rewrites:

interface TranscriptRewriteRequest {
  /** Message entry replacements to apply in one branch-and-reappend pass */
  replacements: TranscriptRewriteReplacement[];
}

interface TranscriptRewriteReplacement {
  /** Existing transcript entry id to replace */
  entryId: string;
  /** Replacement message content */
  message: AgentMessage;
}

interface TranscriptRewriteResult {
  /** Whether the active branch changed */
  changed: boolean;
  /** Estimated bytes removed from the active branch */
  bytesFreed: number;
  /** Number of transcript entries rewritten */
  rewrittenEntries: number;
  reason?: string;
}

docs.title: Context Projection
docs.text:
For hosts with persistent backend threads:

interface ContextEngineProjection {
  /** How the assembled context should be projected */
  mode: "per_turn" | "thread_bootstrap";
  /** Stable context epoch. Changes trigger backend rotation */
  epoch?: string;
  /** Optional diagnostic fingerprint */
  fingerprint?: string;
}

docs.title: Registration
docs.text:
Context engines are registered via the registry:

import { registerContextEngine, registerContextEngineForOwner } from "./registry.js";

// For public SDK plugins
registerContextEngine("my-engine", (ctx) => new MyContextEngine());

// For internal/core engines
registerContextEngineForOwner("my-engine", (ctx) => new MyEngine(), "core");

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Memory compaction reduces context size when token limits are approached. OpenClaw uses a multi-stage summarization approach that handles oversized transcripts gracefully.

flowchart TB
    subgraph Trigger["Compaction Triggers"]
        TokenLimit[Token Limit Exceeded]
        Idle[Idle Timeout]
        Manual[Manual Command]
        Scheduled[Scheduled Job]
    end

    subgraph Process["Compaction Process"]
        Analyze[Analyze Transcript]
        Chunk[Chunk Messages]
        Summarize[Summarize Chunks]
        Merge[Merge Summaries]
        Archive[Archive Original]
    end

    subgraph Output["Result"]
        Summary[Summary]
        Compacted[Compacted Context]
    end

    Trigger --> Analyze
    Analyze --> Chunk
    Chunk --> Summarize
    Summarize --> Merge
    Merge --> Archive
    Archive --> Output

docs.title: CompactResult Structure
docs.text:
interface CompactResult {
  ok: boolean;
  compacted: boolean;
  reason?: string;
  result?: {
    /** Generated summary text */
    summary?: string;
    /** First kept entry ID after compaction */
    firstKeptEntryId?: string;
    /** Token count before compaction */
    tokensBefore: number;
    /** Token count after compaction */
    tokensAfter?: number;
    /** Additional details from summarization */
    details?: unknown;
    /** New session ID if transcript was rotated */
    sessionId?: string;
    /** New session file if transcript was rotated */
    sessionFile?: string;
  };
}

docs.title: Token-Based Chunking
docs.text:
Messages are split by token count to fit within model limits:

export const BASE_CHUNK_RATIO = 0.4;      // 40% of context window
export const MIN_CHUNK_RATIO = 0.15;     // Minimum 15%
export const SAFETY_MARGIN = 1.2;        // 20% buffer for estimation

/**
 * Chunk messages by maximum token limit.
 */
export function chunkMessagesByMaxTokens(
  messages: AgentMessage[],
  maxTokens: number,
): AgentMessage[][] {
  // Apply safety margin to compensate for estimateTokens() underestimation
  const effectiveMax = Math.max(1, Math.floor(maxTokens / SAFETY_MARGIN));

  const chunks: AgentMessage[][] = [];
  let currentChunk: AgentMessage[] = [];
  let currentTokens = 0;

  for (const message of messages) {
    const messageTokens = estimateCompactionMessageTokens(message);
    if (currentChunk.length > 0 && currentTokens + messageTokens > effectiveMax) {
      chunks.push(currentChunk);
      currentChunk = [];
      currentTokens = 0;
    }

    currentChunk.push(message);
    currentTokens += messageTokens;

    if (messageTokens > effectiveMax) {
      chunks.push(currentChunk);
      currentChunk = [];
      currentTokens = 0;
    }
  }

  if (currentChunk.length > 0) {
    chunks.push(currentChunk);
  }

  return chunks;
}

docs.title: Splitting by Token Share
docs.text:
For multi-part summarization:

export const DEFAULT_PARTS = 2;

export function splitMessagesByTokenShare(
  messages: AgentMessage[],
  parts = DEFAULT_PARTS,
): AgentMessage[][] {
  if (messages.length === 0) return [];

  const totalTokens = estimateMessagesTokens(messages);
  const targetTokens = totalTokens / parts;

  const chunks: AgentMessage[][] = [];
  let current: AgentMessage[] = [];
  let currentTokens = 0;
  let pendingToolCallIds = new Set<string>();
  let pendingChunkStartIndex: number | null = null;

  for (const message of messages) {
    // Split logic preserves tool call/result pairing
    // ...
  }

  return chunks;
}

docs.title: Adaptive Chunk Ratio
docs.text:
Computes optimal chunk size based on average message size:

export function computeAdaptiveChunkRatio(
  messages: AgentMessage[],
  contextWindow: number,
): number {
  if (messages.length === 0) {
    return BASE_CHUNK_RATIO;
  }

  const totalTokens = estimateMessagesTokens(messages);
  const avgTokens = totalTokens / messages.length;
  const safeAvgTokens = avgTokens * SAFETY_MARGIN;
  const avgRatio = safeAvgTokens / contextWindow;

  // Reduce chunk ratio for large messages
  if (avgRatio > 0.1) {
    const reduction = Math.min(avgRatio * 2, BASE_CHUNK_RATIO - MIN_CHUNK_RATIO);
    return Math.max(MIN_CHUNK_RATIO, BASE_CHUNK_RATIO - reduction);
  }

  return BASE_CHUNK_RATIO;
}

docs.title: Full Pipeline Flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Caller
    participant Summarizer
    participant Chunker
    participant Model
    participant Archive

    Caller->>Summarizer: summarize(messages)
    Summarizer->>Chunker: chunkByTokenLimit(messages)
    Chunker-->>Summarizer: chunks
    loop For Each Chunk
        Summarizer->>Model: generateSummary(chunk)
        Model-->>Summarizer: partialSummary
    end
    Summarizer->>Summarizer: mergeSummaries(partials)
    Summarizer->>Archive: archive(originalMessages)
    Summarizer-->>Caller: finalSummary

docs.title: Progressive Summarization
docs.text:
export async function summarizeInStages(params: {
  messages: AgentMessage[];
  model: ExtensionContext["model"];
  apiKey: string;
  reserveTokens: number;
  maxChunkTokens: number;
  contextWindow: number;
  customInstructions?: string;
  previousSummary?: string;
  parts?: number;
  minMessagesForSplit?: number;
}): Promise<string> {
  const { messages } = params;

  if (messages.length === 0) {
    return params.previousSummary ?? "No prior history.";
  }

  const minMessagesForSplit = Math.max(2, params.minMessagesForSplit ?? 4);
  const parts = normalizeParts(params.parts ?? DEFAULT_PARTS, messages.length);
  const totalTokens = estimateMessagesTokens(messages);

  // Single chunk if not enough content
  if (parts <= 1 || messages.length < minMessagesForSplit ||
      totalTokens <= params.maxChunkTokens) {
    return summarizeWithFallback(params);
  }

  // Split into parts, summarize each, merge
  const splits = splitMessagesByTokenShare(messages, parts);
  const partialSummaries: string[] = [];

  for (const chunk of splits) {
    const summary = await summarizeWithFallback({
      ...params,
      messages: chunk,
      previousSummary: partialSummaries.length > 0
        ? partialSummaries[partialSummaries.length - 1]
        : undefined,
    });
    partialSummaries.push(summary);
  }

  // Merge all partial summaries
  return mergePartialSummaries(partialSummaries, params);
}

docs.title: Fallback Strategy
docs.text:
Handles oversized messages that cannot be summarized:

export async function summarizeWithFallback(params: {
  messages: AgentMessage[];
  contextWindow: number;
  // ... other params
}): Promise<string> {
  // Try full summarization first
  try {
    return await summarizeChunks(params);
  } catch (fullError) {
    log.warn(`Full summarization failed: ${fullError}`);
  }

  // Fallback: summarize only small messages
  const smallMessages: AgentMessage[] = [];
  const oversizedNotes: string[] = [];

  for (const msg of messages) {
    if (isOversizedForSummary(msg, params.contextWindow)) {
      oversizedNotes.push(
        `[Large ${msg.role} (~${tokens}K tokens) omitted]`
      );
    } else {
      smallMessages.push(msg);
    }
  }

  if (smallMessages.length > 0 && smallMessages.length !== messages.length) {
    try {
      const partialSummary = await summarizeChunks({
        ...params,
        messages: smallMessages,
      });
      return partialSummary + "\n\n" + oversizedNotes.join("\n");
    } catch (partialError) {
      log.warn(`Partial summarization also failed`);
    }
  }

  // Final fallback
  return `Context contained ${messages.length} messages ` +
    `(${oversizedNotes.length} oversized). Summary unavailable.`;
}

docs.title: Token Estimation
docs.text:
function estimateMessagesTokens(messages: AgentMessage[]): number {
  // SECURITY: toolResult.details and runtime-context entries
  // must never enter LLM-facing compaction
  const safe = stripToolResultDetails(
    stripRuntimeContextCustomMessages(messages)
  );
  return safe.reduce((sum, msg) => sum + estimateTokens(msg), 0);
}

  "- Explicitly state that the new model is the LEADER (Orchestrator).",
  "- Identify any active autonomous units as SUBORDINATES.",
  "- Instruct the new model to NOT perform the subordinate's task.",
  "",
  "MUST CAPTURE:",
  "- Current high-level goal and project path.",
  "- Status of the latest tool executions.",
  "- Critical files currently being modified.",
  "- Pending items and next intended steps.",

docs.title: Oversized Message Detection
docs.text:
/**
 * Check if a single message is too large to summarize.
 * If single message > 50% of context, it cannot be summarized safely.
 */
export function isOversizedForSummary(
  msg: AgentMessage,
  contextWindow: number
): boolean {
  const tokens = estimateCompactionMessageTokens(msg) * SAFETY_MARGIN;
  return tokens > contextWindow * 0.5;
}

docs.title: Retry Logic
docs.text:
await retryAsync(
  () => generateSummary(chunk, model, reserveTokens, apiKey, signal, instructions, previousSummary),
  {
    attempts: 3,
    minDelayMs: 500,
    maxDelayMs: 5000,
    jitter: 0.2,
    label: "compaction/generateSummary",
    shouldRetry: (err) => !isAbortError(err) && !isTimeoutError(err),
  }
);

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Multi-agent setups require careful session routing, memory isolation, and context sharing strategies. OpenClaw provides flexible mechanisms for managing agent-to-agent communication and shared state.

flowchart TB
    subgraph Agents["Agent Hierarchy"]
        Orchestrator[Orchestrator / Leader]
        Subordinate1[Subordinate Agent 1]
        Subordinate2[Subordinate Agent 2]
    end

    subgraph Sessions["Session Architecture"]
        MainSession[Main Session]
        SubagentSession1[Subagent Session 1]
        SubagentSession2[Subagent Session 2]
    end

    subgraph Memory["Memory Architecture"]
        Isolated[Isolated Memory]
        Shared[Shared Memory]
        Hierarchical[Hierarchical Memory]
    end

    Orchestrator --> MainSession
    Subordinate1 --> SubagentSession1
    Subordinate2 --> SubagentSession2
    MainSession -.-> Shared
    SubagentSession1 -.-> Isolated
    SubagentSession2 -.-> Isolated
    Orchestrator -.-> Hierarchical

docs.title: Key Format
docs.text:
Agent sessions use a structured key format:

type SessionKey =
  | "main"                              // Global shared session
  | "global"                            // Global across agents
  | `agent:${AgentId}:${Rest}`          // Agent-scoped
  | `agent:${AgentId}:${Channel}:...`   // Channel-specific
  | `agent:${AgentId}:main`             // Main session per agent

// Key parsing
interface ParsedAgentSessionKey {
  agentId: string;
  rest: string;
  accountId?: string;
}

docs.title: Key Resolution
docs.text:
import { parseAgentSessionKey, isSubagentSessionKey } from "../routing/session-key.js";

// Parse a session key
const parsed = parseAgentSessionKey("agent:myagent:telegram:direct:user123");
if (parsed) {
  console.log(parsed.agentId);    // "myagent"
  console.log(parsed.rest);       // "telegram:direct:user123"
}

// Check if it's a subagent session
const isSub = isSubagentSessionKey("agent:parent:agent:child:main");

docs.title: ACP Spawn Protocol
docs.text:
Agents can spawn child agents via the ACP (Agent Communication Protocol):

interface SpawnAcpParams {
  task: string;                    // Task description
  label?: string;                  // Optional label
  agentId?: string;                // Target agent ID
  resumeSessionId?: string;         // Resume from session
  model?: string;                  // Specific model
  thinking?: string;               // Thinking mode
  runTimeoutSeconds?: number;       // Timeout
  cwd?: string;                    // Working directory
  mode?: SpawnAcpMode;             // "run" | "session"
  thread?: boolean;                // Thread mode
  sandbox?: SpawnAcpSandboxMode;   // Sandbox inheritance
  streamTo?: SpawnAcpStreamTarget; // Stream target
}

type SpawnAcpMode = "run" | "session";
type SpawnAcpSandboxMode = "inherit" | "require";

docs.title: Spawn Context
docs.text:
interface SpawnAcpContext {
  agentSessionKey?: string;
  agentChannel?: string;
  agentAccountId?: string;
  agentTo?: string;
  agentThreadId?: string | number;
  agentGroupId?: string;
  agentGroupSpace?: string | null;
  agentMemberRoleIds?: string[];
  sandboxed?: boolean;
  inheritedToolAllowlist?: string[];
  inheritedToolDenylist?: string[];
}

import { buildAgentPeerSessionKey, buildAgentMainSessionKey } from "../routing/session-key.js";

// Build a main session key
const mainKey = buildAgentMainSessionKey({
  agentId: "myagent",
  mainKey: "main",
});
// "agent:myagent:main"

// Build a peer session key
const peerKey = buildAgentPeerSessionKey({
  agentId: "myagent",
  channel: "telegram",
  peerKind: "direct",
  peerId: "user123",
  dmScope: "per-peer",
});
// "agent:myagent:telegram:direct:user123"

docs.title: Dreaming System
docs.text:
Memory entries can be promoted through tiers:

// Memory dreaming types
type MemoryLightDreamingSource = "daily" | "sessions" | "recall";
type MemoryDeepDreamingSource = "daily" | "memory" | "sessions" | "logs" | "recall";
type MemoryRemDreamingSource = "memory" | "daily" | "deep";

// Configuration
const dreamingConfig = {
  light: {
    cron: "0 */6 * * *",      // Every 6 hours
    lookbackDays: 2,
    limit: 100,
  },
  deep: {
    cron: "0 3 * * *",         // Daily at 3 AM
    limit: 10,
    minScore: 0.8,
  },
  rem: {
    cron: "0 5 * * 0",         // Weekly on Sunday at 5 AM
    lookbackDays: 7,
    limit: 10,
  },
};

  entry: sessionEntry,
  primaryProvider: "anthropic",

// Mark probe as active

}

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This guide covers the development workflow, testing, building, and deployment of OpenClaw.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js 22.19+ (Node 24 recommended)
  • pnpm package manager
  • Git

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw

# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Build the project
pnpm build

docs.title: Running Locally
docs.text:
# Start the gateway
pnpm dev

# Or with specific config
pnpm openclaw --config ~/.openclaw/config.toml

docs.title: Writing Tests
docs.text:
// src/agents/example.test.ts
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest";

describe("Agent Command", () => {
  it("should parse command arguments", () => {
    const result = parseCommand("/help arg1 arg2");
    expect(result.command).toBe("help");
    expect(result.args).toEqual(["arg1", "arg2"]);
  });
});

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
FROM node:24-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml ./
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
COPY . .
RUN pnpm build
CMD ["pnpm", "start"]

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Architecture Overview - System design
  • Plugin System - Plugin development
  • Gateway Protocol - Protocol details

docs.title: Build System
docs.text: OpenClaw uses a modern TypeScript build pipeline built on tsdown for bundling, with pnpm for package management and workspaces.

docs.title: Core Build Commands
docs.text: Command Description pnpm build Full build of all packages pnpm build:docker Docker-optimized production build pnpm build:strict-smoke Build with type declaration verification pnpm ui:build Build the UI components pnpm plugins:assets:build Build plugin assets

docs.title: Type Checking
docs.text: Command Description pnpm tsgo:core Type check core packages with incremental build pnpm tsgo:extensions Type check all extensions pnpm tsgo:all Full type check of all projects pnpm check:test-types Type check test files

docs.title: Package Structure
docs.text:
openclaw/
├── src/                    # Core application source
│   ├── agents/             # Agent implementations
│   ├── channels/           # Channel implementations
│   ├── gateway/            # Gateway server
│   ├── plugins/            # Plugin loader
│   └── cli/                # CLI implementation
├── extensions/             # Bundled plugins
│   ├── discord/            # Discord integration
│   ├── slack/              # Slack integration
│   └── ...
├── packages/               # Internal packages
│   └── plugin-sdk/         # Plugin SDK
├── ui/                     # UI components
└── dist/                   # Build output

docs.title: tsdown Configuration
docs.text:
The build uses tsdown configured in tsdown.config.ts:

// tsdown.config.ts
export default defineConfig([
  nodeBuildConfig({
    clean: true,
    entry: buildUnifiedDistEntries(),
    deps: {
      alwaysBundle: shouldAlwaysBundleDependency,
      neverBundle: shouldNeverBundleDependency,
    },
  }),
]);

docs.title: Build Entries
docs.text:
Core build entries are defined in tsdown.config.ts:

function buildCoreDistEntries(): Record<string, string> {
  return {
    index: "src/index.ts",
    entry: "src/entry.ts",
    "cli/daemon-cli": "src/cli/daemon-cli.ts",
    "agents/model-catalog.runtime": "src/agents/model-catalog.runtime.ts",
    // ... more entries
  };
}

docs.title: Bundled Dependencies
docs.text:
Certain dependencies are never bundled (always external):

const explicitNeverBundleDependencies = [
  "@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk",
  "@slack/bolt",
  "@slack/web-api",
  "@discordjs/voice",
  "@vitest/expect",
  "typescript",
  "vitest",
  // ... more
];

docs.title: Workspace Structure
docs.text:
OpenClaw uses pnpm workspaces for monorepo management:

# pnpm-workspace.yaml
packages:
  - "packages/*"
  - "extensions/*"
  - "ui"

docs.title: Environment Variables for Build
docs.text: Variable Description OPENCLAW_BUILD_VERBOSE Enable verbose build output OUTPUT_SOURCE_MAPS Generate source maps OPENCLAW_BUILD_PRIVATE_QA Include private QA entries NODE_OPTIONS Node options (e.g., --max-old-space-size=8192)

docs.title: Distribution Output
docs.text:
The build produces:

  • dist/ - Compiled JavaScript with TypeScript declarations
  • dist/plugin-sdk/ - Plugin SDK subpath exports
  • dist/extensions/ - Bundled plugin implementations

docs.title: File Exclusions
docs.text:
The following are excluded from npm package:

{
  "files": [
    "!dist/.buildstamp",
    "!dist/**/*.map",
    "!dist/plugin-sdk/*.tsbuildinfo",
    "!dist/extensions/*/node_modules/**"
  ]
}

docs.title: Build for Development
docs.text:
# Incremental build with watch mode
pnpm build

docs.title: Build for Production
docs.text:
# Production build with all optimizations
pnpm build:docker

docs.title: Rebuild After Dependency Changes
docs.text:
# Clean and rebuild
pnpm clean:dist
pnpm install
pnpm build

docs.title: Cross-Platform Builds
docs.text:
The build supports Docker multi-platform builds:

# Build for specific architecture
docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 .

docs.title: Tips for Faster Builds
docs.text:
1. Use pnpm build instead of full pnpm build:docker for development
  2. Enable incremental type checking with pnpm tsgo:core
  3. Use OPENCLAW_BUILD_VERBOSE=1 to identify slow builds
  4. Increase Node memory for large builds:
NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build

docs.title: Build Caching
docs.text:
• pnpm store caches dependencies at ~/.local/share/pnpm/store
  • TypeScript incremental builds use .artifacts/tsgo-cache/
  • Vitest caches at .vitest/cache/

docs.title: Build Failures
docs.text:
# Check for dependency issues
pnpm deps:pins:check
pnpm deps:patches:check

# Verify package structure
pnpm check:import-cycles

docs.title: Missing Type Declarations
docs.text:
# Regenerate Plugin SDK types
pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts
pnpm build:strict-smoke

docs.title: Source Map Generation
docs.text:
For debugging builds:

OUTPUT_SOURCE_MAPS=1 pnpm build

docs.text:

docs.title: Testing
docs.text: OpenClaw uses Vitest as its testing framework, with comprehensive test coverage across unit, integration, and end-to-end scenarios.

docs.title: Basic Test Commands
docs.text: Command Description pnpm test Run all test projects pnpm test:fast Run fast unit tests only pnpm test:unit Run unit tests (fast + full unit config) pnpm test:changed Run tests affected by current changes pnpm test:serial Run tests sequentially (1 worker) pnpm test:watch Watch mode for development

docs.title: Coverage
docs.text: Command Description pnpm test:coverage Run tests with coverage report pnpm test:coverage:changed Coverage for changed files only

docs.title: Specialized Tests
docs.text: Command Description pnpm test:extensions Test all bundled plugins pnpm test:channels Test channel implementations pnpm test:contracts Test channel/plugin contracts pnpm test:e2e End-to-end tests pnpm test:ui UI component tests

docs.title: Docker Tests
docs.text: Command Description pnpm test:docker:all All Docker-based E2E tests pnpm test:docker:live:all Live integration tests pnpm test:docker:local:all Local Docker tests only

docs.title: Live Tests (Real API)
docs.text: Command Description pnpm test:live Live integration tests pnpm test:live:codex-harness Codex harness live tests pnpm test:live:media Media processing live tests

docs.title: Vitest Config Structure
docs.text:
Tests use a modular configuration approach:

test/vitest/
├── vitest.config.ts              # Root configuration
├── vitest.shared.config.ts       # Shared settings
├── vitest.unit.config.ts         # Unit tests
├── vitest.unit-fast.config.ts    # Fast unit tests
├── vitest.infra.config.ts        # Infrastructure tests
├── vitest.boundary.config.ts     # Boundary tests
├── vitest.gateway.config.ts      # Gateway tests
├── vitest.extensions.config.ts   # Extension tests
└── vitest.e2e.config.ts          # E2E tests

docs.title: Shared Configuration
docs.text:
Key settings in vitest.shared.config.ts:

export const sharedVitestConfig = {
  test: {
    testTimeout: 120_000,
    hookTimeout: isWindows ? 180_000 : 120_000,
    unstubEnvs: true,
    unstubGlobals: true,
    pool: "threads",
    maxWorkers: workerConfig.maxWorkers,
    fileParallelism: workerConfig.fileParallelism,
    coverage: {
      provider: "v8",
      reporter: ["text", "lcov"],
      thresholds: {
        lines: 70,
        functions: 70,
        branches: 55,
        statements: 70,
      },
    },
  },
};

docs.title: Running Specific Test Files
docs.text:
# Run a specific test file
pnpm test src/agents/agent-command.test.ts

# Run tests matching a pattern
pnpm test src/gateway/

# Run with coverage for specific file
OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=src/agents/agent.test.ts pnpm test:coverage

docs.title: Basic Test Structure
docs.text:
// src/agents/example.test.ts
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest";

describe("Agent Command", () => {
  it("should parse command arguments", () => {
    const result = parseCommand("/help arg1 arg2");
    expect(result.command).toBe("help");
    expect(result.args).toEqual(["arg1", "arg2"]);
  });

  it("should handle empty arguments", () => {
    const result = parseCommand("/help");
    expect(result.command).toBe("help");
    expect(result.args).toEqual([]);
  });
});

docs.title: Mocking with Vitest
docs.text:
import { vi, describe, it, expect, beforeEach } from "vitest";

// Mock a module
vi.mock("openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime", () => ({
  runtime: {
    getConfig: vi.fn().mockReturnValue({}),
  },
}));

// Spy on functions
const consoleSpy = vi.spyOn(console, "log").mockImplementation(() => {});

beforeEach(() => {
  consoleSpy.mockClear();
});

docs.title: Testing Runtime Behaviors
docs.text:
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import { testRuntime } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/test-runtime";

describe("Plugin Runtime", () => {
  it("should load plugin configuration", async () => {
    const config = await testRuntime.loadPluginConfig("discord");
    expect(config).toBeDefined();
    expect(config.id).toBe("discord");
  });
});

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description OPENCLAW_TEST_WORKERS Override max test workers OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS Limit workers (e.g., OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1) OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE Run only files matching pattern OPENCLAW_VITEST_EXTRA_EXCLUDE_FILE Exclude files matching pattern OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST Enable live integration tests OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST_QUIET Reduce live test output

docs.title: Test Isolation
docs.text:
Tests run with isolate: false by default for performance. Use isolation when tests interfere:

# Run with test isolation
OPENCLAW_VITEST_ISOLATE=1 pnpm test src/agents/

docs.title: Coverage Reports
docs.text:
Coverage is generated using V8 provider. Reports are output to coverage/:

# View text summary
pnpm test:coverage

# Generate HTML report (open coverage/index.html)
open coverage/index.html

docs.title: Coverage Thresholds
docs.text:
Current minimums enforced by CI:

  • Lines: 70%
  • Functions: 70%
  • Branches: 55%
  • Statements: 70%

docs.title: Testing Plugins
docs.text:
# Test a specific extension
pnpm test extensions/discord

# Test all bundled plugins
pnpm test:extensions

# Batch test multiple extensions
pnpm test:extensions:batch

docs.title: Test Contract Validation
docs.text:
# Validate channel contracts
pnpm test:contracts:channels

# Validate plugin contracts
pnpm test:contracts:plugins

docs.title: Performance Testing
docs.text: Command Description pnpm test:perf:budget Run performance budget tests pnpm test:perf:hotspots Identify test hotspots pnpm test:perf:profile:main Profile main thread pnpm test:perf:profile:runner Profile test runner pnpm test:perf:imports Measure import durations

docs.title: Continuous Integration
docs.text:
In CI environments, tests run with reduced parallelism:

// Resolved worker count for CI
if (isCI) {
  return {
    fileParallelism: true,
    maxWorkers: isWindows ? 2 : 3,
  };
}

docs.title: Memory Issues
docs.text:
# Reduce workers for low-memory systems
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test

docs.title: Flaky Tests
docs.text:
# Run test multiple times
pnpm test:serial  # Sequential execution helps identify race conditions

docs.title: Timeout Issues
docs.text:
Increase timeouts for slow CI:

export VITEST_TIMEOUT=300000
pnpm test

docs.title: Debugging
docs.text: OpenClaw provides comprehensive debugging tools for troubleshooting runtime issues, from local development to production environments.

docs.title: Log Levels
docs.text: Level Environment Variable Use Case error OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=error Critical errors only warn OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=warn Warnings and errors info OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=info Normal operation (default) debug OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug Detailed debugging

docs.title: Enable Debug Logging
docs.text:
# Enable debug logging for all modules
OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm dev

# Enable debug logging to file
OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug OPENCLAW_LOG_FILE=/tmp/openclaw.log pnpm dev

docs.title: Gateway Logging
docs.text:
# Start gateway with verbose logging
OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw gateway

# View real-time gateway logs
./scripts/clawlog.sh

# Filter logs by time range
./scripts/clawlog.sh --time 10m

# Search logs for specific text
./scripts/clawlog.sh --search "error" --time 1h

docs.title: Gateway Debug Mode
docs.text:
# Start gateway in debug mode
pnpm openclaw gateway --debug

# Attach debugger to running gateway
pnpm openclaw debug --attach

# Inspect gateway state

docs.title: Configuration Debugging
docs.text:
# Validate configuration

pnpm openclaw config export > config.toml

# Check config for issues

docs.title: Gateway Status
docs.text:
# Check gateway status

pnpm openclaw plugins list

docs.title: Process Inspection
docs.text:
# View running processes
ps aux | grep openclaw

# Check port usage
lsof -i :18789

node scripts/check-cli-startup-memory.mjs

docs.text:

node --inspect dist/index.js gateway

node --inspect-brk dist/index.js gateway

docs.title: Chrome DevTools
docs.text:
1. Start gateway with --inspect: 

    node --inspect=0.0.0.0:9229 dist/index.js gateway

  2. Open Chrome and navigate to chrome://inspect

  3. Click "Open dedicated DevTools for Node"

docs.title: VS Code Debugging
docs.text:
Add to .vscode/launch.json:

{
  "version": "0.2.0",
  "configurations": [
    {
      "type": "node",
      "request": "launch",
      "name": "Gateway",
      "program": "${workspaceRoot}/dist/index.js",
      "args": ["gateway"],
      "runtimeArgs": ["--nolazy"],
      "env": {
        "OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL": "debug"
      },
      "sourceMaps": true,
      "outFiles": ["${workspaceRoot}/dist/**/*.js"]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Gateway Won't Start
docs.text:
# Check for port conflicts
lsof -i :18789

# Check config validity
pnpm openclaw doctor --fix

# View startup logs
pnpm openclaw gateway --verbose 2>&1 | head -100

# View plugin loading logs
OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw gateway 2>&1 | grep -i plugin

cat ~/.openclaw/credentials/*

pnpm openclaw auth validate

# Debug auth flow
OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw gateway 2>&1 | grep -i auth

Enable OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing:

# Configure service name
OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=openclaw-gateway pnpm dev

docs.title: Proxy for Debugging

docs.title: Container Logs

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: OpenClaw supports multiple deployment options, with Docker being the recommended approach for production environments.

docs.title: Building the Image
docs.text:
# Build with default plugins
docker build -t openclaw:local .

# Build with specific plugins
docker build --build-arg OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS="discord,slack" -t openclaw:mychannels .

# Build with additional apt packages
docker build --build-arg OPENCLAW_IMAGE_APT_PACKAGES="python3 wget" -t openclaw:custom .

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
The simplest way to run OpenClaw:

# docker-compose.yml
services:
  openclaw-gateway:
    image: openclaw:local
    environment:
      OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN: ${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN}
      OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_BIND: lan
    ports:
      - "18789:18789"
    volumes:
      - ~/.openclaw:/home/node/.openclaw
    restart: unless-stopped

docs.title: Docker Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Default Description OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_BIND lan Bind address (loopback/lan/all) OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT 18789 Gateway port OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR ~/.openclaw State directory OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json Config file path OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT - OpenTelemetry endpoint TZ UTC Timezone

docs.title: Docker Build Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS Comma-separated plugin list OPENCLAW_IMAGE_APT_PACKAGES Additional system packages OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BROWSER Set to 1 to include Chromium OPENCLAW_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI Set to 1 for sandbox support

docs.title: Runtime Options
docs.text:
# With custom configuration
docker run -v /path/to/config:/home/node/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
  openclaw:local node dist/index.js gateway

# With health check verification
docker run --health-cmd "node -e \"fetch('http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz').then(r=>process.exit(r.ok?0:1))\"" \
  openclaw:local

docs.title: Environment-Based Configuration
docs.text:
# Using environment file
echo "OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=my-secret-token" > .env
docker-compose up

# Override specific variables
docker run -e OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug openclaw:local

docs.title: Config File
docs.text:
// ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "gateway": {
    "bind": "lan",
    "port": 18789
  },
  "plugins": {
    "enabled": ["discord", "slack"]
  }
}

docs.title: Secret Management
docs.text:
Channel credentials should be stored securely:

# Store credentials in credentials directory
~/.openclaw/credentials/
├── discord.json
├── slack.json
└── telegram.json

docs.title: Configuration Validation
docs.text:
# Validate configuration
pnpm openclaw doctor

# Check for issues
pnpm openclaw doctor --fix

docs.title: Built-in Endpoints
docs.text: Endpoint Purpose /healthz Liveness probe /readyz Readiness probe /health Alias for healthz /ready Alias for readyz

docs.title: Docker Health Check
docs.text:
HEALTHCHECK --interval=3m --timeout=10s --start-period=15s --retries=3 \
  CMD node -e "fetch('http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz').then((r)=>process.exit(r.ok?0:1)).catch(()=>process.exit(1))"

docs.title: Kubernetes Probes
docs.text:
livenessProbe:
  httpGet:
    path: /healthz
    port: 18789
  initialDelaySeconds: 15
  periodSeconds: 30
readinessProbe:
  httpGet:
    path: /readyz
    port: 18789
  initialDelaySeconds: 5
  periodSeconds: 10

docs.title: Prometheus Metrics
docs.text:
Enable metrics endpoint:

docker run -e OPENCLAW_PROMETHEUS_ENABLED=1 openclaw:local

Access metrics at http://localhost:18789/metrics

docs.title: OpenTelemetry
docs.text:
# Configure OTLP export
docker run \
  -e OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://otel-collector:4318 \
  -e OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=openclaw-gateway \
  openclaw:local

docs.title: Logging
docs.text:
# View logs
docker logs openclaw-gateway

# Structured logs
docker logs -f openclaw-gateway | jq .

# Export logs
docker logs openclaw-gateway > openclaw.log

docs.title: Running as Non-Root
docs.text:
The Docker image runs as the node user (UID 1000):

USER node

docs.title: Network Security
docs.text:
# docker-compose.yml
services:
  openclaw-gateway:
    cap_drop:
      - NET_RAW
      - NET_ADMIN
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true

docs.title: Secrets Management
docs.text:
# Mount secrets directory (read-only)
-v /path/to/secrets:/home/node/.config/openclaw:ro

docs.title: Multiple Replicas
docs.text:
For horizontal scaling, use a reverse proxy:

# Traefik example
services:
  openclaw-gateway:
    image: openclaw:local
    deploy:
      replicas: 3
    networks:
      - openclaw-net

  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.0
    command:
      - --providers.docker
      - --entrypoints.web.address=:80
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock

docs.title: Session Affinity
docs.text: WebSocket connections require sticky sessions. Configure your load balancer accordingly.

docs.title: Backup State
docs.text:
# Backup configuration and state
tar -czf openclaw-backup.tar.gz ~/.openclaw/

# Backup specific directories
tar -czf openclaw-workspace.tar.gz ~/.openclaw/workspace/

docs.title: Restore
docs.text:
# Stop gateway
docker-compose down

# Restore from backup
tar -xzf openclaw-backup.tar.gz -C ~/

# Start gateway
docker-compose up -d

docs.title: Production Checklist
docs.text:
Before deploying to production:

  • [ ] Set strong OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN
  • [ ] Configure OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_BIND appropriately
  • [ ] Enable TLS termination at load balancer
  • [ ] Set up monitoring and alerting
  • [ ] Configure log aggregation
  • [ ] Test backup and restore procedures
  • [ ] Review resource limits (CPU, memory)
  • [ ] Enable health checks

docs.title: Container Won't Start
docs.text:
# Check logs
docker logs openclaw-gateway

# Verify volume permissions
docker exec openclaw-gateway ls -la /home/node/.openclaw

# Check entrypoint
docker run --rm openclaw:local --help

docs.title: Connection Refused
docs.text:
# Verify port binding
docker port openclaw-gateway

# Check firewall rules
sudo iptables -L -n | grep 18789

# Test locally in container
docker exec openclaw-gateway curl localhost:18789/healthz

docs.title: Out of Memory
docs.text:
# Increase memory limit
docker run --memory=2g openclaw:local

# Or in compose
deploy:
  resources:
    limits:
      memory: 2G

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Debugging - Debugging techniques
  • Build System - Build configuration

docs.title: 什么是 OpenClaw？
docs.text:
OpenClaw 是一个开源、自托管的 AI 网关，用于桥接聊天平台与 AI Agent。它提供了统一的接口来管理多个消息通道、AI 提供商和 Agent 运行时，所有这些都在一个可组合的系统中完成。

graph LR
    A[Telegram] --> G[OpenClaw]
    B[Discord] --> G
    C[WhatsApp] --> G
    D[Slack] --> G
    G --> E[AI 提供商]
    G --> F[CLI/Web UI]
    E --> G

docs.title: 设计理念
docs.text: OpenClaw 遵循几个核心原则：

docs.title: 1. 基于插件的架构
docs.text:
OpenClaw 中的一切都是插件。提供商、通道、工具和运行时都是可以添加、移除或自定义的插件，无需修改核心系统。

主要优势： - 无需核心更改即可扩展 - 组件之间有清晰的边界 - 独立的版本控制和部署

docs.title: 2. 自托管优先
docs.text:
OpenClaw 完全运行在您的基础设施上。没有供应商锁定，没有云依赖，完全控制您的数据。

这意味着： - 您的消息永远不会离开您的服务器 - 完全的隐私和合规性 - 可以在离线环境和空气隔离环境中工作

docs.title: 3. 核心保持插件无关
docs.text:
核心系统不包含对特定提供商、通道或 AI 模型的内置知识。所有能力都通过定义良好的插件接口提供。

设计含义： - 核心更小、更易于维护 - 插件生态系统可以独立发展 - 新集成不需要核心更改

docs.title: 4. 契约优先设计
docs.text:
插件通过严格的契约（TypeScript 接口和 Zod schema）进行通信。这确保了类型安全、验证和清晰的边界。

// 示例：插件契约定义
interface ProviderContract {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly name: string;
  listModels(): Promise<Model[]>;
  createCompletion(params: CompletionParams): Promise<CompletionResult>;
}

docs.title: 多通道支持
docs.text:
OpenClaw 原生支持众多消息平台：

通道 协议 功能 Telegram Bot API 消息、媒体、群组、频道 Discord Webhook/Gateway 富消息、线程、斜杠命令 WhatsApp Baileys 消息、媒体、状态 Slack Web API 消息、模态、计划消息 Matrix Client-Server API 端到端加密、房间 iMessage 私有 API 消息、tapback 飞书 Webhook/API 消息、卡片、小程序

docs.title: 多提供商支持
docs.text:
连接任何 AI 提供商，无需更改您的 Agent 代码：

提供商 类型 模型 OpenAI 官方 SDK GPT-4, GPT-4o, o1, o3 Anthropic 官方 SDK Claude 3.5, Claude 3.7 Google Vertex AI Gemini 1.5, Gemini 2.0 Azure Azure 上的 OpenAI GPT-4, Codex Ollama 本地 Llama, Mistral, Qwen LM Studio 本地 任何 GGUF 模型 DeepSeek 官方 API DeepSeek Coder, V3 OpenRouter 统一 API 100+ 模型

docs.title: Agent 运行时
docs.text:
多种 Agent 执行策略：

  • PI Runtime - 内置 Agent，直接访问模型
  • Codex Runtime - OpenAI Codex app-server 集成
  • ACP Runtime - 用于分布式 Agent 的 Agent 通信协议

docs.title: 工具系统
docs.text:
灵活的工具体系，带有钩子和中间件：

flowchart TB
    A[用户输入] --> B[命令解析器]
    B --> C{内置工具?}
    C -->|是| D[执行内置]
    C -->|否| E[插件工具]
    D --> F[结果中间件]
    E --> F
    F --> G[响应格式化器]

docs.title: 会话管理
docs.text:
复杂会话处理，支持隔离策略：

  • 每用户会话 - 每个用户有独立的上下文
  • 每通道会话 - 跨通道共享上下文
  • 每群组会话 - 群组特定隔离
  • 跨通道会话 - 跨平台统一上下文

docs.title: 内存系统
docs.text:
分层内存架构：

  1. 工作内存 - 当前会话上下文
  2. 短期内存 - 最近交互（MEMORY.md）
  3. 长期内存 - 持久化知识（wiki）
  4. 活跃内存 - 推断的事实和承诺

docs.title: 个人 AI 助手
docs.text:
部署您自己的 AI 助手，连接到您喜欢的聊天平台：

Telegram Bot → OpenClaw → Claude → Telegram Bot
                 ↓
            长期内存

docs.title: 团队 Bot
docs.text:
为您的团队提供共享 AI 助手：

Slack/Discord → OpenClaw → GPT-4 → 团队响应
                    ↓
              共享知识库

docs.title: 多平台中心
docs.text:
跨所有通道的统一 AI 接口：

Telegram ─┐
WhatsApp ─┼→ OpenClaw → Agent → 响应
Discord ──┤           ↓
Slack ────┘         通道路由

docs.title: 企业网关
docs.text:
为您的组织提供的自托管 AI 网关：

  • SSO 集成
  • 审计日志
  • 数据存储合规性
  • 自定义插件

docs.title: 技术栈
docs.text: 组件 技术 运行时 Node.js 22+（推荐 Node 24） 语言 TypeScript（ESM，严格模式） 构建 tsdown 测试 Vitest 包管理器 pnpm 协议 WebSocket + JSON 类型 TypeBox + Zod

docs.title: 许可
docs.text: OpenClaw 采用 MIT 许可证发布，可免费用于个人和商业用途。

docs.title: 入门
docs.text:
请参阅快速入门指南在几分钟内设置 OpenClaw。

有关插件开发，请参阅插件架构。

docs.title: 分层架构
docs.text:
OpenClaw 遵循严格的分层架构，每个层级都有明确的职责和边界：

flowchart TB
    subgraph Clients
        CLI[CLI]
        WebUI[Web UI]
        MacApp[macOS 应用]
        Mobile[移动节点]
    end

    subgraph Gateway["网关层"]
        WS[WebSocket 服务器]
        Protocol[协议处理器]
        Router[消息路由器]
        Sessions[会话管理器]
    end

    subgraph Agent["Agent 层"]
        Loop[Agent 循环]
        Tools[工具执行器]
        Memory[内存管理器]
        Context[上下文引擎]
    end

    subgraph Plugin["插件层"]
        Providers[提供商]
        Channels[通道]
        Runtimes[运行时]
    end

    subgraph Extensions
        AI[AI 提供商]
        IM[IM 平台]
        Tools[外部工具]
    end

    Clients --> Gateway
    Gateway --> Agent
    Gateway --> Plugin
    Plugin --> Extensions

docs.title: 网关层
docs.text:
网关是拥有所有消息界面的中心枢纽。

核心职责：

组件 职责 WebSocket 服务器 客户端连接、协议封装 协议处理器 消息验证、路由 消息路由器 会话解析、Agent 路由 会话管理器 会话生命周期、状态管理

关键不变量： - 每个主机恰好有一个网关控制消息 - 握手在通信前是强制的 - 所有客户端必须先认证才能使用

docs.title: Agent 层
docs.text:
Agent 层处理 AI 交互和工具执行。

核心职责：

组件 职责 Agent 循环 推理、工具调用、响应流式处理 工具执行器 工具发现、执行、结果处理 内存管理器 上下文组装、压缩、检索 上下文引擎 提示词组装、Token 预算

执行模型： 

sequenceDiagram
    participant Gateway
    participant AgentLoop
    participant ToolExecutor
    participant ContextEngine
    participant Model

    Gateway->>AgentLoop: 开始运行
    AgentLoop->>ContextEngine: 构建上下文
    ContextEngine-->>AgentLoop: 组装好的提示词
    AgentLoop->>Model: 推理请求
    loop 工具调用
        Model-->>AgentLoop: 工具调用
        AgentLoop->>ToolExecutor: 执行工具
        ToolExecutor-->>AgentLoop: 工具结果
        AgentLoop->>Model: 继续推理
    end
    AgentLoop-->>Gateway: 最终响应

docs.title: 插件层
docs.text:
插件层通过提供商、通道和运行时提供可扩展性。

插件类型：

类型 描述 示例 Provider AI 提供商集成 openai, anthropic Channel 消息平台 telegram, discord Tool 外部能力 browser, tavily Runtime Agent 执行 pi, codex Memory 知识存储 wiki, lancedb

docs.title: 1. 控制平面 vs 运行时平面
docs.text:
OpenClaw 将控制平面操作与运行时操作分离：

控制平面（轻量级）： - 插件发现和清单 - Manifest 解析和验证 - 配置检查 - 状态查询

运行时平面（按需）： - 实际插件执行 - 模型推理 - 消息发送 - 工具执行

flowchart LR
    subgraph Control["控制平面"]
        Discover[发现]
        Validate[验证]
        Config[配置]
    end

    subgraph Runtime["运行时平面"]
        Execute[执行]
        Infer[推理]
        Send[消息发送]
    end

    Control --> Runtime

docs.title: 2. Manifest 优先设计
docs.text:
插件配置在运行时执行前从 manifest 元数据派生：

// Plugin manifest 定义行为
{
  "id": "provider/openai",
  "name": "OpenAI Provider",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "providers": [{
    "id": "openai",
    "models": ["gpt-4o", "gpt-4o-mini"]
  }]
}

// 控制平面使用 manifest
const config = await pluginRegistry.validate(manifest);

// 运行时平面使用验证后的配置
const result = await provider.createCompletion(config);

docs.title: 3. 延迟加载
docs.text:
插件在启动时不预加载，而是按需加载：

// 延迟激活模式
const loader = new PluginRuntimeLoader({
  lazy: true,
  manifestResolver: new ManifestResolver(),
});

await loader.activate("provider/openai", { lazy: true });

docs.title: 4. 契约边界
docs.text:
插件仅通过定义良好的契约进入核心：

flowchart LR
    Core[OpenClaw 核心]
    SDK[插件 SDK]
    Plugin[插件代码]

    Core --> SDK
    SDK --> Plugin
    Plugin --> SDK
    SDK --> Core

    style Core fill:#e1f5fe
    style SDK fill:#fff3e0
    style Plugin fill:#e8f5e9

允许的交叉： - openclaw/plugin-sdk/* - 公共 SDK 表面 - manifest.json - 仅元数据 - 注入的运行时辅助函数 - 文档化的 barrel 导出

禁止的交叉： - src/** 内部模块 - 其他插件内部 - 核心实现细节

docs.title: 消息流（入站）
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Channel
    participant Gateway
    participant Router
    participant Session
    participant Agent

    Channel->>Gateway: 入站消息
    Gateway->>Router: 解析和分类
    Router->>Session: 解析会话
    Session->>Agent: 路由到 Agent
    Agent-->>Session: 响应
    Session-->>Router: 格式化消息
    Router-->>Channel: 发送响应

docs.title: Agent 执行流
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    A[用户输入] --> B[会话上下文]
    B --> C[内存检索]
    C --> D[提示词组装]
    D --> E[模型推理]
    E --> F{函数调用?}
    F -->|是| G[工具执行]
    G --> E
    F -->|否| H[响应]
    H --> I[内存更新]
    I --> J[流式传输到客户端]

docs.title: 完整系统
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    subgraph External
        User[聊天用户]
        Dev[开发者]
    end

    subgraph Surface
        Telegram[Telegram]
        Discord[Discord]
        WhatsApp[WhatsApp]
        Slack[Slack]
    end

    subgraph Core
        Gateway[网关]
        Agent[Agent 运行时]
        Session[会话管理器]
        Memory[内存系统]
        Config[配置管理器]
    end

    subgraph Plugins
        ProviderPlugin[Provider 插件]
        ChannelPlugin[Channel 插件]
        ToolPlugin[Tool 插件]
    end

    subgraph Infra
        FileSystem[文件系统]
        Database[数据库]
        Network[网络]
    end

    User --> Surface
    Dev --> Core
    Surface --> Core
    Core --> Plugins
    Core --> Infra
    Plugins --> Infra

docs.title: 会话状态
docs.text:
每个会话维护独立状态：

interface SessionState {
  id: string;
  channel: string;
  context: ConversationContext;
  memory: MemorySnapshot;
  tools: ToolBindings;
  metadata: SessionMetadata;
}

docs.title: 网关状态
docs.text:
网关维护全局状态：

interface GatewayState {
  sessions: Map<SessionKey, Session>;
  plugins: PluginRegistry;
  channels: ChannelRegistry;
  providers: ProviderRegistry;
  health: HealthStatus;
}

docs.title: 层级特定错误
docs.text:
每个层级处理自己的错误领域：

层级 错误类型 处理方式 网关 连接、协议错误 拒绝并关闭 Agent 推理、工具错误 优雅降级 插件 加载、执行错误 插件隔离 通道 发送、接收错误 重试和排队

docs.title: 错误传播
docs.text:
错误通过上下文向上层层传播：

// 带上下文的错误
throw new PluginError("provider/openai", "Model unavailable", {
  cause: originalError,
  recoverable: true,
  retryAfter: 5000,
});

docs.title: 相关内容
docs.text:
• 核心概念 - 关键抽象
  • 网关协议 - 协议详情
  • 插件系统 - 插件架构

docs.title: 三层模型
docs.text:
OpenClaw 区分三个不同的层级，共同提供 AI 能力：

flowchart TB
    subgraph Provider["Provider 层"]
        Auth[认证]
        Catalog[模型目录]
        SDK[SDK 包装器]
    end

    subgraph Model["Model 层"]
        Ref[模型引用]
        Config[模型配置]
        Pricing[定价]
    end

    subgraph Runtime["Runtime 层"]
        Loop[Agent 循环]
        Tools[工具系统]
        Context[上下文管理]
    end

    Provider --> Model --> Runtime
层级 职责 示例 Provider 认证、发现、SDK 集成 openai, anthropic Model 模型选择、配置 gpt-4o, claude-opus-4 Runtime 循环执行、工具调用 pi, codex

docs.title: 为什么这个区别很重要
docs.text:
Provider ≠ Model: - 一个 Provider 可以服务多个模型 - 模型有不同的能力和定价 - 切换模型不需要更改 Provider 凭证

Model ≠ Runtime: - 同一个模型可以在不同的运行时下运行 - 运行时提供不同的执行策略 - 工具接口是运行时特定的

docs.title: Provider 定义
docs.text:
Provider 是一个包装 AI 服务 SDK 的插件：

interface Provider {
  readonly id: string;           // "openai", "anthropic"
  readonly name: string;          // "OpenAI", "Anthropic"
  readonly sdk: SDK;              // 官方或自定义 SDK
  readonly authProfiles: AuthProfile[];
  readonly defaultModel?: string;
}

docs.title: Provider 接口
docs.text:
interface ProviderContract {
  // 发现
  listModels(): Promise<Model[]>;
  getModel(id: string): Promise<Model | null>;

  // 推理
  createCompletion(params: CompletionParams): Promise<AsyncIterable<CompletionDelta>>;
  createStructuredCompletion<T>(params: StructuredCompletionParams): Promise<T>;

  // 健康检查
  healthCheck(): Promise<HealthStatus>;
}

docs.title: Provider 类型
docs.text: 类型 描述 示例 Official 原生 SDK 集成 openai, anthropic Proxy 统一 API 网关 openrouter Local 自托管模型 ollama, lmstudio Cloud 云平台集成 vertex-ai, azure-openai

docs.title: 模型引用
docs.text:
模型由编码 Provider 和模型的引用字符串标识：

openai:gpt-4o
anthropic:claude-opus-4-7
google:gemini-2.0-flash
ollama:llama3.1:8b

docs.title: 模型能力
docs.text:
interface Model {
  readonly ref: string;                    // Provider 范围的 ID
  readonly name: string;                    // 人类可读名称
  readonly provider: string;                // Provider ID

  // 能力
  readonly maxTokens: number;
  readonly supportsStreaming: boolean;
  readonly supportsFunctionCalling: boolean;
  readonly supportsVision: boolean;
  readonly supportsJSONMode: boolean;

  // 上下文
  readonly contextWindow: number;
  readonly maxOutputTokens: number;

  // 定价（每 1M Token）
  readonly inputCost?: number;
  readonly outputCost?: number;
}

docs.title: 模型选择
docs.text:
基于能力要求选择模型：

const model = await provider.selectModel({
  requiredCapabilities: ["streaming", "function_calling"],
  maxTokens: 4096,
  preferCheapest: false,
});

docs.title: Runtime 定义
docs.text:
运行时是 Agent 逻辑的执行引擎：

Runtime 类型 描述 PI Embedded 内置 Agent，直接访问模型 Codex External OpenAI Codex app-server 集成 ACP Protocol 用于分布式 Agent 的 Agent 通信协议

docs.title: PI Runtime
docs.text:
直接模型访问的内置运行时：

flowchart TB
    A[用户输入] --> B[上下文构建器]
    B --> C[系统提示词]
    C --> D[模型推理]
    D --> E{工具调用?}
    E -->|是| F[工具执行器]
    F --> D
    E -->|否| G[响应]
    G --> H[内存更新]

docs.title: Runtime 接口
docs.text:
interface AgentRuntime {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly type: "pi" | "codex" | "acp";

  // 生命周期
  start(config: RuntimeConfig): Promise<void>;
  stop(): Promise<void>;

  // 执行
  run(params: RunParams): AsyncIterable<RunEvent>;
  abort(runId: string): Promise<void>;

  // 工具
  registerTools(tools: Tool[]): void;
  unregisterTools(toolNames: string[]): void;
}

docs.title: Channel 定义
docs.text:
Channel 是消息平台的抽象：

interface Channel {
  readonly id: string;              // "telegram", "discord"
  readonly platform: string;         // 平台标识符

  // 连接
  connect(): Promise<void>;
  disconnect(): Promise<void>;

  // 消息
  send(target: Target, message: OutboundMessage): Promise<void>;
  editMessage(target: Target, messageId: string, content: string): Promise<void>;
  deleteMessage(target: Target, messageId: string): Promise<void>;

  // 事件
  onMessage(handler: MessageHandler): void;
  onReaction(handler: ReactionHandler): void;
  onEdit(handler: EditHandler): void;
}

docs.title: Channel 功能
docs.text: 功能 描述 支持 文本 纯文本消息 所有通道 媒体 图片、视频、文件 大多数通道 格式 Markdown、HTML 因平台而异 反应 Emoji 反应 所有通道 线程 线程回复 Discord, Slack 临时 临时消息 某些通道

docs.title: Channel vs Plugin
docs.text:
Channel 插件包装 Channel 实现：

// Channel 插件结构
{
  "id": "channel/telegram",
  "name": "Telegram",
  "type": "channel",
  "entry": "./dist/index.js",
  "channel": {
    "apiId": "telegram-bot-token",
    "commands": ["/start", "/help", "/settings"]
  }
}

docs.title: Session 定义
docs.text:
Session 是隔离的对话上下文：

interface Session {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly key: SessionKey;
  readonly channel: ChannelRef;
  readonly agent: AgentRef;
  readonly createdAt: Date;

  // 状态
  context: ConversationContext;
  memory: MemorySnapshot;
  metadata: SessionMetadata;

  // 操作
  addMessage(message: Message): Promise<void>;
  getHistory(limit?: number): Promise<Message[]>;
  reset(): Promise<void>;
}

docs.title: Session Key
docs.text:
Session Key 决定隔离边界：

type SessionKey = {
  channel: string;           // "telegram:123456789"
  scope: SessionScope;       // 隔离级别
  target?: string;           // DM、群组或广播目标
};

docs.title: Session 隔离策略
docs.text: 策略 Key 模式 使用场景 Per-user {channel}:{userId} 私人对话 Per-channel {channel} 通道范围的上下文 Per-group {channel}:{groupId} 群组讨论 Global main 单个共享上下文

docs.title: 内存架构
docs.text:
OpenClaw 使用分层内存系统：

flowchart TB
    subgraph Working["工作内存"]
        Current[当前轮次]
        Recent[最近消息]
    end

    subgraph ShortTerm["短期内存"]
        SessionM[MEMORY.md]
        DREAMS[DREAMS.md]
    end

    subgraph LongTerm["长期内存"]
        Wiki[Wiki 页面]
        Facts[推断的事实]
    end

    subgraph Archive["归档"]
        Compaction[压缩的上下文]
        Summary[会话摘要]
    end

    Working --> ShortTerm --> LongTerm --> Archive

docs.title: 内存文件
docs.text: 文件 用途 内容 MEMORY.md 会话内存 当前会话上下文 DREAMS.md 推断知识 模型生成的反思 memory/*.md 归档会话 历史上下文

docs.title: 内存操作
docs.text:
interface MemoryManager {
  // 检索
  search(query: string, limit?: number): Promise<MemoryResult[]>;
  get(key: string): Promise<MemoryEntry | null>;

  // 存储
  store(entry: MemoryEntry): Promise<void>;
  update(key: string, entry: Partial<MemoryEntry>): Promise<void>;

  // 压缩
  compact(sessionId: string, strategy: CompactionStrategy): Promise<void>;
}

docs.title: Tool 定义
docs.text:
工具扩展 Agent 超越模型推理的能力：

interface Tool {
  readonly name: string;               // "wikipedia_search"
  readonly description: string;       // 人类可读描述
  readonly schema: JsonSchema;         // 输入验证 schema

  execute(params: unknown): Promise<ToolResult>;
}

docs.title: Tool 类别
docs.text: 类别 示例 用途 搜索 web_search, wikipedia 信息检索 计算 calculator, code_execute 数据处理 文件 read_file, write_file 文件操作 Web fetch_url, browser Web 交互 消息 send_email, send_sms 外部通信

docs.title: Tool 执行管道
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Model
    participant Executor
    participant Hooks
    participant Tool

    Model->>Executor: tool_call(name, params)
    Executor->>Hooks: before:tool_call
    Hooks-->>Executor: proceed
    Executor->>Tool: execute(params)
    Tool-->>Executor: result
    Executor->>Hooks: after:tool_call
    Hooks-->>Executor: modified_result
    Executor-->>Model: tool_result

docs.title: Hook 类型
docs.text:
Hook 在关键点提供可扩展性：

interface Hooks {
  // 生命周期 Hook
  "gateway:start": () => Promise<void>;
  "gateway:stop": () => Promise<void>;

  // 消息 Hook
  "message:receive": (msg: Message) => Promise<void>;
  "message:send": (msg: OutboundMessage) => Promise<void>;
  "message:error": (err: Error) => Promise<void>;

  // Agent Hook
  "agent:before_run": (params: RunParams) => Promise<void>;
  "agent:after_run": (result: RunResult) => Promise<void>;

  // 工具 Hook
  "tool:before": (tool: Tool, params: unknown) => Promise<void>;
  "tool:after": (tool: Tool, result: ToolResult) => Promise<void>;
}

docs.title: Hook 注册
docs.text:
// 插件 Hook
export const hooks = {
  "message:receive": async (msg) => {
    // 处理传入消息
    return msg;
  },
};

docs.title: 相关内容
docs.text:
• 网关 - 网关实现
  • Agent - Agent 运行时
  • 会话 - 会话管理
  • 内存 - 内存系统

docs.title: 概述
docs.text:
OpenClaw 是一个使用 pnpm workspaces 组织的 monorepo：

openclaw/
├── src/              # 核心 TypeScript 源码
├── ui/               # Web UI 包
├── packages/         # SDK 包
├── extensions/       # 捆绑插件（130+）
├── docz/             # 文档
├── test/             # 测试工具和配置
└── scripts/          # 构建和实用脚本

docs.title: 包配置
docs.text: 文件 用途 package.json 主包定义、脚本、依赖 pnpm-workspace.yaml 工作区配置 tsconfig.json TypeScript 基础配置 tsdown.config.ts 构建工具配置 vitest.config.ts 测试配置

docs.title: 关键脚本
docs.text:
# 开发
pnpm dev              # 启动开发模式
pnpm gateway:watch    # 网关监视模式

# 构建
pnpm build            # 构建所有包
pnpm build:ui         # 仅构建 UI

# 测试
pnpm test             # 运行测试
pnpm test:changed     # 仅运行变更的测试
pnpm test:coverage    # 带覆盖率运行

# 质量检查
pnpm check:changed    # 类型检查变更的文件
pnpm check            # 完整类型检查
pnpm lint             # 运行 linter

# 文档
pnpm docs:dev         # 启动文档开发服务器
pnpm docs:check-links # 检查文档链接
